reasoning:
As of March 2, 2025, Alina Kashlinskaya is ranked exactly #12 on the FIDE top women's list with a rating of 2493. The players immediately below her are very close (Mariya Muzychuk is at 2490, a mere 3-point gap; Vaishali is at 2484), whereas the player above her (Shuvalova) is at 2508, a 15-point gap. This asymmetrical spacing means the immediate mathematical likelihood of dropping out of the top 12 is much greater than moving up if points fluctuate. 

For 2025-03-09, since FIDE only updates rankings monthly on the 1st of the month, the March FIDE list will still be fully in effect, meaning Wikipedia will almost certainly still display her as #12 (resolving YES). I assess this at 95%. 
For 2025-04-01, the April FIDE rankings will be published, but there is a partial chance Wikipedia updates a day late, maintaining her YES resolution artificially, plus some chance she naturally holds her rank if both she and those immediately behind her are inactive. I assess this at 60%.
For late 2025 and early 2026, the long-term trend dominates. As a somewhat surprise entry to her current peak position, mean-reversion and the dense pack of players trailing by only a few points suggests it's slightly more probable than not that she drops below #12. I assess the late 2025 dates at around 45-50%, slowly tracking towards 42% for the year-out forecast as uncertainty grows and settles near her long-term base-rate probability of maintaining peak position.
